title: The Printing Press Revolution
author: From: Johannes Gutenberg
content: The democratization of information always leads to decentralization of power. If you have unique knowledge, share it to build your own influence.
action: Transform internal knowledge into an external resource.

title: The Industrial Fatigue
author: From: The Luddite Riots
content: The resistance was not to the technology, but to the loss of agency over their own craft. When you automate, ensure you are still the architect of the work.
action: Reinvest time saved by automation into creative work.

title: The Age of Exploration
author: From: Magellan’s Circumnavigation
content: The cost of exploration is high, but the cost of stagnation is total loss of relevance. The greatest danger is staying in the harbor.
action: Research a new methodology to expand your horizons.

title: The Athenian Assembly
author: From: Ancient Greece
content: Public discourse requires that people listen more than they talk. In the assembly, the silent majority often held the true pulse of the state.
action: Listen fully before adding your perspective.

title: The Viking Expansion
author: From: Norse Sagas
content: They were not just raiders; they were the most sophisticated traders and boat-builders of their time. Preparation (the boat) was 90% of the conquest.
action: Dedicate time to sharpening your tools.
